commit.message:
feat: configurable target profiles + AID threshold experiments (EXP-1511-1518)

Target profile system for production_therapy.py:
- TargetProfile dataclass with configurable thresholds and safety behavior
- Built-in profiles: ADA Clinical, AID-Aware, Pregnancy, Pediatric
- CLI: --profile ada|aid|pregnancy|pediatric
- Profile-aware safety tiers, recommendations, and report display
- AID-aware profile: sustained-only TBR for safety override, CV relaxed to 40%

EXP-1511-1518 empirical validation (8 experiments, 11 patients):
- EXP-1511: 53% of TBR is sustained (>45min), only 10% brief (<15min)
- EXP-1512: 70% of hypo time is AID-managed (median 87%)
- EXP-1513: AID-managed lows recover 40% faster (70min vs 115min)
- EXP-1514: Duration-weighted TBR reshuffles risk rankings
- EXP-1515: Sustained TBR threshold sweep confirms 4% is well-placed
- EXP-1516: CV correlates with TAR (r=0.67) not TBR (r=0.37)
- EXP-1517: AID targets don't improve grades — ADA targets validated
- EXP-1518: Brief lows predict sustained lows (r=0.60) — warning signal

Key conclusion: ADA TBR<4% is appropriate even for AID users.
Patient k (95% TIR, 4.8% TBR) safety alert is justified — 49% sustained,
slowest recovery in cohort (121min), only 52% AID-managed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

revert: switch secrets back to queue-time variables (templateParameters corrupt values)

ADO templateParameters expansion injects values inline into YAML before
parsing. Special characters in the secret (backslash escape sequences,
YAML metacharacters) are transformed during YAML parsing, delivering a
corrupted value to bash — causing AADSTS7000215 even when the correct
secret is provided.

Queue-time variables bypass YAML parsing entirely: ADO sets env vars
directly from its secure variable store when $(VAR) is used in env:
blocks. This matches what the user manually tested and confirmed works.

Changes:
- Remove parameters: block from pipeline YAML
- Replace all "${{ raw }}...${{ endraw }}" env: values with $(VAR)
- Update header comment

commit.message:
Fix overlay byte capacity init mismatch (#1119)

Initialize FlowControl byte capacity to INITIAL_PEER_FLOOD_READING_CAPACITY_BYTES
(300,000) instead of flow_control_bytes_batch_size (100,000). stellar-core's
FlowControl constructor uses getFlowControlBytesTotal() which returns 300,000
by default, matching the SEND_MORE_EXTENDED grant sent to peers. The previous
3x mismatch meant peers operating within their granted budget could exhaust
the local node's byte capacity and be disconnected.

commit.message:
fix(task-116): ensure finalize always runs when Copilot CLI step fails (#145)

Without `continue-on-error: true`, a Copilot CLI failure (wrong package,
bad token, unavailable binary) silently skips the `Finalize superseding
PR workflow` step — leaving the original Dependabot PR in an ambiguous
state with no comment and no close.

## Change

Added `continue-on-error: true` to the `Run Copilot repair agent` step:

```yaml
- name: Run Copilot repair agent
  if: steps.preflight.outputs.ready == 'true'
  continue-on-error: true   # ← ensures finalize always runs
  env:
    ...
```

When the Copilot step fails, the result file is absent → `load_result()`
returns a `defer` decision → `comment_manual_review()` posts a graceful
comment on the original PR. The existing fallback path in `finalize`
handles this correctly; it just wasn't reachable before.

---------

Reject small-order Curve25519 public keys in ECDH (#1091)

overlay auth handshake path (auth.rs:420) since it uses x25519-dalek directly.

Curve25519.cpp:60-63, which rejects small-order points via libsodium.

Fix emoji aspect ratio: use char_aspect=1.0 for emoji charset

emoji per row was already halved (out_width // 2), but char_aspect=2.0
was still applied when deriving row count, causing output to be half as
tall as expected. Emoji cells are approximately square (2 cols × 2 rows),
so char_aspect=1.0 is correct.

commit.message:
fix: replace ${#VAR} with wc -c to avoid Nunjucks comment parse error

Bash string-length syntax ${#VARNAME} contains the sequence {# which
Nunjucks interprets as the start of a comment block. Without a closing
#} it throws "expected end of comment, got end of file" and the
scaffolder fails before generating any files.

Replace with $(echo -n "$VAR" | wc -c) which is equivalent but
contains no Nunjucks-special character sequences.

commit.message:
Support POSIX character classes via \p{...} property syntax (#116)

Wire up the 13 JDK POSIX character classes (Lower, Upper, ASCII, Alpha,
Digit, Alnum, Punct, Graph, Print, Blank, Cntrl, XDigit, Space) so they
are recognized by the \p{...} and \P{...} property escape syntax.

The POSIX range tables already existed in UnicodeTables (for the
[[:lower:]] bracket syntax) but were not reachable from the \p{Lower}
code path. Add a POSIX_PROPERTY_GROUPS map keyed by the JDK property
names and look it up in Parser.lookupUnicodeGroup().

Add comprehensive tests (PosixPropertyClassTest) covering:
- Cross-check against JDK for representative inputs on all 13 classes
- Exhaustive ASCII scan (all 128 code points) for every class
- Negation via \P{...}
- Usage inside character classes and compound patterns

Part of #112

Implement UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS flag and add flag test coverage (#115)

Fix UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS (Pattern flag 256) which was silently broken:
the flag mapped to ParseFlags.UNICODE_GROUPS which is already in the
LIKE_PERL baseline, making it a complete no-op. Now \d, \w, \s, and \b
use proper Unicode definitions when the flag is set.

Implementation:
- Add ParseFlags.UNICODE_CHAR_CLASS (bit 12) separate from UNICODE_GROUPS
- Add Unicode Perl group tables to UnicodeTables: unicodeDigit() (Nd),
  unicodeSpace() (White_Space property), unicodeWord() (L+M+Nd+Nl+Pc+Join_Control)
- Update Parser.maybeParsePerlCCEscape() to use Unicode tables when flag set
- Add EmptyOp.UNICODE_WORD_BOUNDARY and UNICODE_NON_WORD_BOUNDARY flags
- Update Compiler to emit Unicode word boundary ops
- Update NFA, DFA, BitState, and OnePass engines for Unicode word boundaries
- DFA: add FLAG_LAST_UNICODE_WORD state flag, widen emptyFlags mask to 9 bits,
  handle Unicode word boundary in deferred assertion expansion

Add comprehensive test coverage for under-tested Pattern flags:
- LiteralFlagTest: 20 tests for LITERAL flag behavior
- UnicodeCaseTest: 14 tests for UNICODE_CASE (documents SafeRE divergence)
- UnicodeCharClassTest: 28 tests for UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS (\d, \s, \w, \b)
- Update EmptyOpTest and ParseFlagsTest for new flag bits

Also update AGENTS.md: file issues for bugs found during other work.

Refs #108

commit.message:
🐛 Fix flaky Rakefile spec caused by project_root resolving wrong dir

The 'writes Rakefile from example' spec runs in a tmpdir with a
minimal gemspec, but configure_template_tokens! resolved project_root
via Rake.application.original_dir — pointing at the real kettle-jem
repo root. Depending on spec ordering, Gem::Specification.load could
fail on the real gemspec due to stale loader state, causing
'Gem name could not be derived' errors (~60% failure rate).

Fix: set @@project_root_override to the tmpdir so gemspec_metadata
finds the test's minimal gemspec, matching the approach already used
by the neighboring error-mode spec.

commit.message:
Fix SSH key negotiation error for legacy iOS devices

Add -o HostKeyAlgorithms=+ssh-rsa to all SSH/SCP commands to support
legacy iOS devices that only offer ssh-rsa and ssh-dss key types.
Modern SSH clients have disabled these by default for security, but
legacy jailbroken iOS devices require this support.

Fixes: 'no matching host key type found' errors when connecting to
older iOS devices over SSH/SCP.

sec: enforce privilege check before session mutation in EctDisplayAction

Move the hasPrivilege check to run immediately after extracting and
validating demographicNo, before any session.setAttribute calls.
Previously, EctSessionBean and eChartID were written to the HTTP session
(lines 160-161) before the _demographic privilege check at line 169,
allowing unauthorized requests to mutate session state and trigger
setUpEncounterPage side effects without authorization.

Introduce a rebuildBean flag to share the extracted/validated
demographicNo between the privilege check and the bean-building block,
avoiding code duplication.

Fixes unresolved review comment from PR #1483 (thread 24).
